definition: Commodity supercycle positioning is crowded, creating near-term downside vulnerability. Net commodity overweights at +1.8σ, 13/15 sell-side desks structurally bullish, copper managed money longs at 89th percentile. However, crowded positioning signals short-term vulnerability, not structural thesis invalidity — the 2000s supercycle was widely recognized by 2005 and still ran for years.
evidence: Copper managed money longs at 89th percentile
CFTC positioning and ETF flow data confirm moderate-to-elevated crowding
Pair_3 identified this as a critical blind spot in risk_analyst_01's framework
2000s supercycle was widely recognized by 2005 yet still ran for years
open_questions: At what positioning level does crowding become self-defeating vs. merely creating interim volatility?
Can systematic/CTA positioning be distinguished from fundamental positioning for signal quality?
